explicit_text: Karin Svenson Berg was my great-grandmother on my mother's side, and she was a remarkable woman educated at the University of Lund in Sweden. In the late 1800s, she was a schoolteacher in her hometown of Osby and planned to marry Andrew Berg, who had immigrated to America. After Andrew died in Minnesota, she married his brother Nils and settled in Wahkon, Minnesota. I have many treasured items that belonged to her, including a basket box she carried from Sweden and gifts from her schoolchildren in 1902. Her story is a cherished part of my family history and immigrant heritage.
